name: code-documentation-code-explain
description: You are a code education expert specializing in explaining complex code through clear narratives, visual diagrams, and step-by-step breakdowns. Transform difficult concepts into understandable explanations.
capabilities: Analyze code complexity and identify key programming concepts
Generate Mermaid flow diagrams showing code execution flow
Create UML-style class diagrams for object-oriented code
Provide step-by-step progressive explanations from simple to complex
Visualize algorithm execution with detailed step-by-step traces
Generate interactive code examples for hands-on learning
author: sickn33
securitySummary: Static analysis flagged 71 patterns in markdown documentation containing educational Python code examples. All findings are false positives - the detected patterns (shell commands, URLs, crypto references) exist only in code samples for teaching purposes, not in executable skill logic. The skill is a documentation and code explanation tool with no actual command execution, network access, or filesystem operations.
valueStatement: Developers struggle to understand complex codebases and onboard quickly. This skill transforms difficult code into clear explanations with visual diagrams, step-by-step breakdowns, and interactive examples.
userTitle: Explain Complex Code with Visual Diagrams
limitations: Cannot execute or test code - provides explanations only
Diagram generation limited to Mermaid and ASCII formats
Best suited for Python, JavaScript, and common languages
Cannot access external documentation or APIs for context

useCases.targetUser: Engineering managers and team leads onboarding new developers
useCases.title: Developer Onboarding
useCases.description: Help new team members understand existing codebase structure, key components, and design patterns through visual diagrams and progressive explanations.

useCases.targetUser: Students and self-taught developers learning computer science fundamentals
useCases.title: Learning Complex Algorithms
useCases.description: Break down sorting algorithms, recursion, and data structures with step-by-step visualizations showing each execution state.

useCases.targetUser: Senior developers conducting code reviews and writing technical documentation
useCases.title: Code Review Documentation
useCases.description: Generate clear explanations of complex pull requests, highlighting design patterns used, potential pitfalls, and best practice recommendations.

promptTemplates.title: Basic Code Explanation
promptTemplates.prompt: Explain this code section: [paste code]. Include a high-level summary and identify the key programming concepts used.
promptTemplates.scenario: Quick understanding of unfamiliar code encountered during development

promptTemplates.title: Flow Diagram Generation
promptTemplates.prompt: Analyze this function and generate a Mermaid flow diagram showing the execution flow, decision points, and function calls: [paste code]
promptTemplates.scenario: Visualizing complex function logic for documentation or presentations

promptTemplates.title: Algorithm Visualization
promptTemplates.prompt: Walk through this algorithm step by step with a visualization showing how the data changes at each iteration: [paste sorting/searching algorithm]
promptTemplates.scenario: Learning or teaching how algorithms process data

promptTemplates.title: Design Pattern Analysis
promptTemplates.prompt: Identify design patterns in this codebase and explain each with a class diagram, benefits, drawbacks, and alternative approaches: [paste code]
promptTemplates.scenario: Understanding architectural decisions and preparing technical documentation

bestPractices: Start with high-level overview before diving into implementation details
Use visual diagrams alongside text explanations for better comprehension
Provide progressive complexity - beginner explanation first, then advanced details
antiPatterns: Explaining code line-by-line without establishing overall purpose first
Using jargon without defining terms for the target audience level
Providing only text explanations when visual diagrams would clarify flow

faq.question: What programming languages does this skill support?
faq.answer: The skill works best with Python and JavaScript due to extensive pattern libraries, but can explain code in most mainstream languages including Java, Go, Rust, and TypeScript.

faq.question: Can this skill generate diagrams for any code?
faq.answer: Diagrams are generated in Mermaid format which renders in most markdown viewers. Flow diagrams, class diagrams, and sequence diagrams are supported. Complex nested logic may require simplified representations.

faq.question: Does this skill execute or test the code it analyzes?
faq.answer: No, this skill only analyzes and explains code statically. It does not execute, run, or test code. All explanations are based on static analysis of the code structure.

faq.question: How detailed are the step-by-step explanations?
faq.answer: Explanations are progressive - starting with a simple overview, then drilling into key components. You can request more detail on specific sections or ask for beginner/advanced level explanations.

faq.question: Can this skill help with debugging understanding?
faq.answer: Yes, the skill can explain why bugs occur by tracing execution flow, identifying edge cases, and highlighting common pitfalls in the code structure.

faq.question: Is this skill suitable for complete programming beginners?
faq.answer: Yes, explanations include simple analogies and progressively build complexity. Specify your experience level for appropriately targeted explanations.
